descriptor.tagline: guest-room scopes what an agent is allowed to do, and refuses everything else by construction — not by a reminder you hope it reads.
descriptor.what: I built it for my own work. I wanted an agent that did the task and didn't get distracted or reach for things I never granted it. guest-room makes "exactly these capabilities, nothing ambient" a real, testable object.

claims.claim: A room grants exactly its doors and denies the rest, by name
Attenuation is append-only — authority never widens
A capability dies with its lease and never exceeds its ceiling
The engine names no guest, so it works for any agent
A door's authority is the same object across unix/vsock/tcp wires
The algebra holds for EVERY case, not just examples (bounded-exhaustive)
A tcp/vsock door requires a per-launch token; unauthorized peers reach no handler
A per-request HMAC door rejects tampered, wrong-key, and replayed requests
A denied request is NEVER forwarded upstream — over real unix sockets and chains
A signed grant is honored only if its Ed25519 signature verifies against the issuer key

descriptor.positioning: capability allowlisting for agents via socket brokers
